Guideline: Use const constructors for immutable widgets
Description: Const constructors allow Flutter to reuse widget instances improving performance
Do: Mark widgets as const when all parameters are compile-time constants
Don't: Create new widget instances unnecessarily

Description: Build methods should only construct the widget tree without side effects
Do: Return widget tree based only on current state and props
Don't: Call setState or perform async operations in build
Code Bad: @override Widget build(BuildContext context) { fetchData(); return Text(data); }

Description: Break down complex widgets into smaller focused widget classes for reusability
Do: Create custom widget classes for reusable UI components
Don't: Build everything in one massive build method
Code Good: class TraderCard extends StatelessWidget { final Trader trader; const TraderCard({required this.trader}); @override Widget build(BuildContext context) => Card(child: ...); }

Description: StatelessWidget is more efficient when the widget does not need to manage state
Do: Use StatelessWidget for widgets that only depend on constructor parameters
Don't: Use StatefulWidget when no internal state is needed
Code Good: class GreetingWidget extends StatelessWidget { final String name; const GreetingWidget({required this.name}); @override Widget build(context) => Text('Hello $name'); }
Code Bad: class GreetingWidget extends StatefulWidget { final String name; ... } // No internal state used

Description: Compose widgets by combining smaller widgets rather than extending existing ones
Do: Build complex widgets by composing simpler widgets
Don't: Create deep widget inheritance hierarchies
Code Good: class PrimaryButton extends StatelessWidget { @override Widget build(context) => ElevatedButton(style: primaryStyle, ...); }
Code Bad: class PrimaryButton extends ElevatedButton { // Overriding internal behavior }

Description: Call setState only when state actually changes and include only state changes inside
Do: Call setState with minimal state changes and after validating the change
Don't: Call setState unnecessarily or with heavy computations inside

Description: Choose the right state management solution based on app complexity
Do: Use Provider/Riverpod for most apps and Bloc for complex business logic
Don't: Use setState for everything in a complex app
Code Bad: class _MyAppState extends State<MyApp> { int globalCounter = 0; String userName = ''; // All state in root }

Description: Place state at the lowest common ancestor that needs access to it
Do: Keep state close to where it is used and lift only when necessary
Don't: Put all state at the app root for convenience
Code Good: class _SearchPageState { String _query = ''; } // State stays in search page
Code Bad: class _AppState { String searchQuery = ''; String userName = ''; String theme = ''; } // All state in root

Guideline: Use ValueNotifier for simple reactive state
Description: ValueNotifier provides simple reactive state management without additional packages
Do: Use ValueNotifier with ValueListenableBuilder for simple local state
Don't: Import heavy state management packages for trivial state
Code Good: final counter = ValueNotifier<int>(0); ValueListenableBuilder(valueListenable: counter, builder: (ctx, value, _) => Text('$value'))
Code Bad: // Using Bloc for a simple counter with no business logic

Description: Global mutable state makes apps hard to test and reason about
Do: Use dependency injection and scoped state management
Don't: Create global variables for app state

Description: Understand the difference between Flexible and Expanded for proper flex layouts
Do: Use Expanded when child should fill available space and Flexible for proportional sizing
Don't: Confuse Flexible and Expanded or nest them incorrectly

Description: Ensure widgets have bounded height and width constraints to prevent overflow
Do: Wrap scrollable content in containers with defined constraints
Don't: Put ListView directly inside Column without constraints

Description: LayoutBuilder provides parent constraints allowing responsive widget building
Do: Use LayoutBuilder to adapt UI based on available space
Don't: Hardcode sizes that only work on specific screen sizes
Code Good: LayoutBuilder(builder: (context, constraints) { return constraints.maxWidth > 600 ? WideLayout() : NarrowLayout(); })
Code Bad: Container(width: 400) // Fixed width ignoring screen size

Description: SafeArea protects content from notches status bars and system UI
Do: Wrap main content in SafeArea to respect system insets
Don't: Ignore safe area insets causing content to be hidden
Code Bad: Scaffold(body: MyContent()) // May be hidden by notch

Description: SizedBox is more efficient than Container for simple spacing
Do: Use SizedBox for fixed spacing between widgets
Don't: Use Container with only width or height set

Description: ListView.builder lazily builds children providing better performance for long lists
Do: Use ListView.builder for lists with many items
Don't: Use ListView with children array for dynamic lists

Guideline: Implement itemExtent for fixed height items
Description: Providing itemExtent allows Flutter to skip measuring items improving scroll performance
Do: Set itemExtent when all list items have the same height
Don't: Skip itemExtent for lists with many fixed-height items

Guideline: Use keys for stateful list items
Description: Keys help Flutter track items correctly when the list changes
Do: Add keys to list items that have state or can be reordered
Don't: Omit keys from lists that can be modified
Code Bad: ListView.builder(itemBuilder: (ctx, i) => ListTile()) // No keys for dynamic list

Description: Load data in pages to reduce memory usage and improve initial load time
Do: Use infinite scroll or explicit pagination for large datasets
Don't: Load thousands of items at once
Code Good: ListView.builder(itemBuilder: (ctx, i) { if (i >= items.length - 5) loadMore(); return ItemTile(items[i]); })

Description: Slivers provide more control for complex scrolling scenarios with multiple scroll sections
Do: Use CustomScrollView with Slivers for headers sticky items and mixed content
Don't: Force complex scroll behaviors with nested ListViews

Guideline: Use named routes with typed arguments
Description: Named routes improve code organization and enable deep linking
Do: Define routes centrally with typed route arguments
Don't: Hardcode MaterialPageRoute throughout the app
Code Good: Navigator.pushNamed(context, '/trader', arguments: TraderId(123)); // With route generation

Description: Navigator 2.0 provides declarative navigation for complex routing needs
Do: Use go_router or auto_route for apps with complex navigation requirements
Don't: Use Navigator 2.0 for simple linear navigation
Code Bad: // 200 lines of custom RouteInformationParser for simple navigation

Description: Override back button behavior when needed for confirmation or custom logic
Do: Use WillPopScope to intercept back navigation when needed
Don't: Ignore back navigation edge cases
Code Good: WillPopScope(onWillPop: () async { return await showConfirmDialog(context); }, child: Form())
Code Bad: Form() // No confirmation when leaving with unsaved changes

Description: Use typed arguments and validate route parameters
Do: Define argument classes and validate in route builders
Don't: Pass untyped Map or dynamic data between routes
Code Good: class TraderArgs { final String id; final String name; } Navigator.pushNamed(ctx, '/trader', arguments: TraderArgs(id: '1', name: 'Top'));

Description: FutureBuilder handles async data loading with proper state handling
Do: Create Future outside build and handle all connection states
Don't: Create Future inside build causing infinite rebuilds
Code Good: late Future<Data> _future; @override void initState() { _future = fetchData(); } Widget build(ctx) => FutureBuilder(future: _future, ...)
Code Bad: Widget build(ctx) => FutureBuilder(future: fetchData(), ...) // New future each build

Description: StreamBuilder rebuilds when stream emits new data ideal for real-time updates
Do: Use StreamBuilder for data that changes over time like WebSocket or database streams
Don't: Use FutureBuilder and manually refresh for real-time data
Code Bad: // Polling with Timer and FutureBuilder for real-time prices

Description: Prevent memory leaks by canceling stream subscriptions and timers
Do: Cancel subscriptions in dispose method
Don't: Leave subscriptions active after widget disposal

Description: Provide feedback during async operations and handle failures gracefully
Do: Show loading indicators and error messages for all async operations
Don't: Show blank screen or crash on async errors
Code Good: if (snapshot.connectionState == ConnectionState.waiting) return Spinner(); if (snapshot.hasError) return ErrorWidget(snapshot.error);
Code Bad: if (snapshot.hasData) return DataWidget(snapshot.data); // No loading or error handling

Description: Define app theme centrally and access via Theme.of(context)
Do: Define colors fonts and styles in ThemeData
Don't: Hardcode colors and styles throughout widgets

Description: Implement both light and dark themes for better user experience and accessibility
Do: Define separate ThemeData for light and dark modes
Don't: Ignore dark mode or hardcode light colors
Code Good: MaterialApp(theme: lightTheme, darkTheme: darkTheme, themeMode: ThemeMode.system)
Code Bad: MaterialApp(theme: ThemeData.light()) // No dark mode support

Description: Define colors by their purpose rather than their appearance
Do: Create color schemes with semantic names like primary surface error
Don't: Name colors by their literal values like blue400
Code Bad: final buttonColor = Color(0xFF2196F3); // Magic color value

Guideline: Use implicit animations for simple cases
Description: Implicit animations like AnimatedContainer handle animation automatically
Do: Use AnimatedFoo widgets for simple property animations
Don't: Use explicit animation controllers for simple opacity changes
Code Bad: // Using AnimationController just to fade a widget

Description: AnimationController must be disposed to prevent memory leaks
Do: Dispose controllers in the State dispose method
Don't: Forget to dispose AnimationControllers

Description: Animation curves make motion feel more natural and polished
Do: Apply appropriate Curves to animations for natural motion
Don't: Use linear animation for all movements

Description: Complex animations can cause jank if not optimized properly
Do: Use RepaintBoundary and optimize build during animation
Don't: Rebuild entire widget tree on each animation frame
Code Bad: // Rebuilding entire page on each animation frame

Description: Form widget provides validation and save functionality for multiple fields
Do: Wrap form fields in Form widget with a GlobalKey
Don't: Validate each field independently without Form

Description: Validate form input before submission with clear error messages
Do: Add validators to form fields with helpful error messages
Don't: Submit forms without validation

Description: Manage keyboard appearance and dismissal for better UX
Do: Handle keyboard dismissal and focus changes appropriately
Don't: Let keyboard obscure form fields
Code Good: Scaffold(body: GestureDetector(onTap: () => FocusScope.of(context).unfocus(), child: SingleChildScrollView(child: Form())))
Code Bad: Scaffold(body: Form()) // Keyboard may obscure fields

Description: Guide users through form fields with proper input actions and focus management
Do: Set textInputAction and handle onFieldSubmitted to move focus
Don't: Use default actions and let users tap each field manually
Code Bad: TextFormField() // User must tap each field

Description: Reduce unnecessary widget rebuilds by structuring widget tree properly
Do: Split widgets and use const constructors to minimize rebuild scope
Don't: Rebuild entire screens for small state changes
Code Good: const Header(), Consumer<Counter>(builder: (_, counter, __) => Text('${counter.value}')), const Footer()

Description: RepaintBoundary isolates painting reducing repaint scope for complex widgets
Do: Wrap complex or frequently updating widgets in RepaintBoundary
Don't: Wrap every widget in RepaintBoundary

Description: Use Flutter DevTools to identify performance issues and memory leaks
Do: Profile your app in profile mode to find bottlenecks
Don't: Guess at performance issues without profiling
Code Good: // Run: flutter run --profile and use DevTools Performance tab

Description: Load appropriately sized images and cache effectively
Do: Use cacheWidth cacheHeight and proper image providers
Don't: Load full resolution images for small displays

Description: Provide meaningful labels for screen readers on important elements
Do: Add Semantics widget or semanticLabel property for accessibility
Don't: Ship without accessibility labels
Code Good: IconButton(icon: Icon(Icons.delete), tooltip: 'Delete item', onPressed: ...) Semantics(label: 'User avatar', child: CircleAvatar())

Description: Test and ensure your app works with TalkBack and VoiceOver
Do: Test navigation and content reading with screen readers enabled
Don't: Ignore screen reader testing
Code Good: Semantics(label: 'Price chart showing upward trend', child: ChartWidget())

Description: Ensure text and interactive elements have sufficient color contrast
Do: Test contrast ratios meet WCAG guidelines
Don't: Use low contrast color combinations
Code Good: Text('Important', style: TextStyle(color: Colors.black)) // On white background
Code Bad: Text('Important', style: TextStyle(color: Colors.grey[400])) // On grey background

Description: Test widgets in isolation to catch UI regressions
Do: Write widget tests for important UI components
Don't: Skip widget testing entirely
Code Good: testWidgets('TraderCard shows name', (tester) async { await tester.pumpWidget(TraderCard(trader)); expect(find.text('TopTrader'), findsOneWidget); });

Guideline: Use golden tests for visual regression
Description: Golden tests catch unintended visual changes by comparing screenshots
Do: Add golden tests for important UI components
Don't: Rely only on manual visual testing
Code Good: testWidgets('Button matches golden', (tester) async { await tester.pumpWidget(MyButton()); await expectLater(find.byType(MyButton), matchesGoldenFile('button.png')); });

Description: Isolate units under test by mocking external dependencies
Do: Use mockito or mocktail to mock services and repositories
Don't: Test with real network calls and databases

Description: Hot reload accelerates development by instantly showing changes
Do: Structure code to maximize hot reload effectiveness
Don't: Rely on full restart for all changes
Code Good: // Extract widgets and use const to improve hot reload; Run: r for hot reload

Description: Account for iOS and Android platform differences in your app
Do: Use Platform checks or adaptive widgets for platform-specific behavior
Don't: Ignore platform conventions entirely
Code Bad: // Same button style ignoring platform conventions

Description: Choose well-maintained packages with good community support
Do: Check package scores maintenance and issue activity
Don't: Add packages without evaluating quality
Code Good: // Check pub.dev score, GitHub issues, last update before adding
Code Bad: // Adding any package without checking quality
